heading: Standards; monitoring of Head Start agencies and programs
label: 9836a Standards; monitoring of Head Start agencies and programs

Effective DateSection effective , but not applicable to Head Start agencies and other recipients of financial assistance under the Head Start Act ( et seq.) until , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1994 Amendment note under .May 18, 1994Oct. 1, 199442 U.S.C. 9831section 127 of Pub. L. 103–252section 9832 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: performance standards with respect to services required to be provided, including health, parental involvement, nutritional, and social services, transition activities described in , and other services;section 9837a of this title

children.label: i
children.text: language knowledge and skills, including oral language and listening comprehension;

children.label: ii
children.text: literacy knowledge and skills, including phonological awareness, print awareness and skills, and alphabetic knowledge;

children.label: iii
children.text: mathematics knowledge and skills;

children.label: iv
children.text: science knowledge and skills;

children.label: v
children.text: cognitive abilities related to academic achievement and child development;

children.label: vi
children.text: approaches to learning related to child development and early learning;

children.label: vii
children.text: social and emotional development related to early learning, school success, and social problemsolving;

children.label: viii
children.text: abilities in creative arts;

children.label: ix
children.text: physical development; and

children.label: x
children.text: in the case of limited English proficient children, progress toward acquisition of the English language while making meaningful progress in attaining the knowledge, skills, abilities, and development described in clauses (i) through (ix), including progress made through the use of culturally and linguistically appropriate instructional services;

children.label: C
children.text: administrative and financial management standards;

children.label: i
children.text: shall meet or exceed State and local requirements concerning licensing for such facilities; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall be accessible by State and local authorities for purposes of monitoring and ensuring compliance, unless State or local laws prohibit such access; and

children.label: E
children.text: such other standards as the Secretary finds to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with experts in the fields of child development, early childhood education, child health care, family services (including linguistically and culturally appropriate services to non-English speaking children and their families), administration, and financial management, and with persons with experience in the operation of Head Start programs;

children.label: i
children.text: past experience with use of the standards in effect under this subchapter on ;December 12, 2007

children.label: ii
children.text: changes over the period since , in the circumstances and problems typically facing children and families served by Head Start agencies;October 27, 1998

children.label: iii
children.text: recommendations from the study on Developmental Outcomes and Assessments for Young Children by the National Academy of Sciences, consistent with ;section 9844(j) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: developments concerning research-based practices with respect to early childhood education and development, children with disabilities, homeless children, children in foster care, and family services, and best practices with respect to program administration and financial management;

children.label: v
children.text: projected needs of an expanding Head Start program;

children.label: vi
children.text: guidelines and standards that promote child health services and physical development, including participation in outdoor activity that supports children’s motor development and overall health and nutrition;

children.label: vii
children.text: changes in the characteristics of the population of children who are eligible to participate in Head Start programs, including country of origin, language background, and family structure of such children, and changes in the population and number of such children who are in foster care or are homeless children;

children.label: viii
children.text: mechanisms to ensure that children participating in Head Start programs make a successful transition to the schools that the children will be attending;

children.label: ix
children.text: the need for Head Start agencies to maintain regular communications with parents, including conducting periodic meetings to discuss the progress of individual children in Head Start programs; and

children.label: x
children.text: the unique challenges faced by individual programs, including those programs that are seasonal or short term and those programs that serve rural populations;

children.label: i
children.text: review and revise as necessary the standards in effect under this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure that any such revisions in the standards will not result in the elimination of or any reduction in quality, scope, or types of health, educational, parental involvement, nutritional, social, or other services required to be provided under such standards as in effect on ; andDecember 12, 2007

children.label: D
children.text: consult with Indian tribes, including Alaska Natives, experts in Indian, including Alaska Native, early childhood education and development, linguists, and the National Indian Head Start Directors Association on the review and promulgation of standards under paragraph (1) (including standards for language acquisition and school readiness).

children.heading: Standards relating to obligations to delegate agencies
children.label: 3 Standards relating to obligations to delegate agencies
children.text: In developing any modifications to standards under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall describe the obligations of a Head Start agency to a delegate agency to which the Head Start agency has delegated responsibility for providing services under this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: classroom instructional practices;

children.label: B
children.text: identification of children with special needs;

children.label: C
children.text: program evaluation; and

children.label: D
children.text: administrative and financial management practices.

children.label: A
children.text: be developmentally, linguistically, and culturally appropriate for the population served;

children.label: B
children.text: be reviewed periodically, based on advances in the science of early childhood development;

children.label: C
children.text: be consistent with relevant, nationally recognized professional and technical standards related to the assessment of young children;

children.label: D
children.text: be valid and reliable in the language in which they are administered;

children.label: E
children.text: be administered by staff with appropriate training for such administration;

children.label: F
children.text: provide for appropriate accommodations for children with disabilities and children who are limited English proficient;

children.label: G
children.text: be high-quality research-based measures that have been demonstrated to assist with the purposes for which they were devised; and

children.label: H
children.text: be adaptable, as appropriate, for use in the self-assessment of Head Start agencies, including in the evaluation of administrative and financial management practices.

children.heading: Use of measures; limitations on use
children.label: 3 Use of measures; limitations on use
i
children.text: helping to develop the skills, knowledge, abilities, and development described in subsection (a)(1)(B) of children participating in Head Start programs, with an emphasis on measuring skills that scientifically valid research has demonstrated are related to children’s school readiness and later success in school;

children.label: ii
children.text: improving classroom practices, including reviewing children’s strengths and weaknesses and individualizing instruction to better meet the needs of the children involved;

children.label: iii
children.text: identifying the special needs of children; and

children.label: iv
children.text: improving overall program performance in order to help programs identify problem areas that may require additional training and technical assistance resources.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: B Limitations
children.text: Such measures shall not be used to exclude children from Head Start programs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, through regulation, shall ensure the confidentiality of any personally identifiable data, information, and records collected or maintained under this subchapter by the Secretary and any Head Start agency. Such regulations shall provide the policies, protections, and rights equivalent to those provided to a parent, student, or educational agency or institution under .section 1232g of title 20

children.heading: Prohibition on nationwide database
children.label: B Prohibition on nationwide database
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to authorize the development of a nationwide database of personally identifiable data, information, or records on children resulting from the use of measures under this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: ranking, comparing, or otherwise evaluating individual children for purposes other than research, training, or technical assistance; and

children.label: ii
children.text: providing rewards or sanctions for individual children or teachers.

children.heading: Results
children.label: B Results
children.text: The Secretary shall not use the results of a single assessment as the sole method for assessing program effectiveness or making agency funding determinations at the national, regional, or local level under this subchapter.

children.heading: Monitoring of local agencies and programs
children.label: c Monitoring of local agencies and programs
A
children.text: A full review, including the use of a risk-based assessment approach, of each such agency at least once during each 3-year period.

children.label: B
children.text: A review of each newly designated Head Start agency immediately after the completion of the first year such agency carries out a Head Start program.

children.label: i
children.text: return visits to Head Start agencies with 1 or more findings of deficiencies, not later than 6 months after the Secretary provides notification of such findings, or not later than 12 months after such notification if the Secretary determines that additional time is necessary for an agency to address such a deficiency prior to the review; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a review of Head Start agencies with significant areas of noncompliance.

children.label: D
children.text: Other reviews, including unannounced site inspections of Head Start centers, as appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: other early childhood education and development programs, personnel management, financial accountability, and systems development and monitoring; and

children.label: II
children.text: the diverse (including linguistic and cultural) needs of eligible children (including children with disabilities, homeless children, children in foster care, and limited English proficient children) and their families;

children.label: ii
children.text: include, to the maximum extent practicable, current or former employees of the Department of Health and Human Services who are knowledgeable about Head Start programs; and

children.label: iii
children.text: shall receive periodic training to ensure quality and consistency across reviews;

children.label: B
children.text: include as part of the reviews, a review and assessment of program strengths and areas in need of improvement;

children.label: C
children.text: include as part of the reviews, a review and assessment of whether programs have adequately addressed population and community needs (including those of limited English proficient children and children of migrant or seasonal farmworker families);

children.label: D
children.text: include as part of the reviews, an assessment of the extent to which the programs address the communitywide strategic planning and needs assessment described in ;section 9835(g)(1)(C) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: include information on the innovative and effective efforts of the Head Start agencies to collaborate with the entities providing early childhood and development services or programs in the community and any barriers to such collaboration that the agencies encounter;

children.label: F
children.text: include as part of the reviews, a valid and reliable research-based observational instrument, implemented by qualified individuals with demonstrated reliability, that assesses classroom quality, including assessing multiple dimensions of teacher-child interactions that are linked to positive child development and later achievement;

children.label: G
children.text: are conducted in a manner that evaluates program performance, quality, and overall operations with consistency and objectivity, are based on a transparent and reliable system of review, and are conducted in a manner that includes periodic interrater reliability checks, to ensure quality and consistency, across and within regions, of the reviews and of noncompliance and deficiency determinations;

children.label: H
children.text: in the case of reviews of Early Head Start agencies and programs, are conducted by a review team that includes individuals who are knowledgeable about the development of infants and toddlers;

children.label: i
children.text: using Federal funds appropriately;

children.label: ii
children.text: using Federal funds specifically to purchase property (consistent with ) and to compensate personnel;section 9839(f) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: securing and using qualified financial officer support; and

children.label: iv
children.text: reporting financial information and implementing appropriate internal controls to safeguard Federal funds;

children.label: J
children.text: include as part of the reviews of the programs, a review and assessment of whether the programs are in conformity with the eligibility requirements under , including regulations promulgated under such section and whether the programs have met the requirements for the outreach and enrollment policies and procedures, and selection criteria, in such section, for the participation of children in programs assisted under this subchapter;section 9840(a)(1) of this title

children.label: K
children.text: include as part of the reviews, a review and assessment of whether agencies have adequately addressed the needs of children with disabilities, including whether the agencies involved have met the 10 percent minimum enrollment requirement specified in and whether the agencies have made sufficient efforts to collaborate with State and local agencies providing services under section 619 or part C of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (, 1431 et seq.); andsection 9835(d) of this title20 U.S.C. 1419

children.label: L
children.text: include as part of the reviews, a review and assessment of child outcomes and performance as they relate to agency-determined school readiness goals described in subsection (g)(2), consistent with subsection (b)(5).

children.heading: Standards relating to obligations to delegate agencies
children.label: 3 Standards relating to obligations to delegate agencies
children.text: In conducting a review described in paragraph (1)(A) of a Head Start agency, the Secretary shall determine whether the agency complies with the obligations described in subsection (a)(3). The Secretary shall consider such compliance in determining whether to renew financial assistance to the Head Start agency under this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: be presented to the agency in a timely, transparent, and uniform manner that conveys information of program strengths and weaknesses and assists with program improvement; and

children.label: B
children.text: be used by the agency to inform the development and implementation of its plan for training and technical assistance.

children.heading: Evaluations and corrective action for delegate agencies
children.label: d Evaluations and corrective action for delegate agencies
A
children.text: procedures for evaluating delegate agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: procedures for defunding delegate agencies; and

children.label: C
children.text: procedures for a delegate agency to appeal a defunding decision.

children.label: A
children.text: shall evaluate its delegate agencies using the procedures established under this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall inform the delegate agencies of the deficiencies identified through the evaluation that are required to be corrected.

children.label: A
children.text: initiating procedures to terminate the designation of the agency unless the agency corrects the deficiency;

children.label: B
children.text: conducting monthly monitoring visits to such delegate agency until all deficiencies are corrected or the Head Start agency decides to defund such delegate agency; and

children.label: i
children.text: only as reimbursements except that, upon receiving a request from the delegate agency accompanied by assurances satisfactory to the Head Start agency that the funds will be appropriately safeguarded, the Head Start agency shall provide to the delegate agency a working capital advance in an amount sufficient to cover the estimated expenses involved during an agreed upon disbursing cycle; and

children.label: ii
children.text: only if there is continuity of services.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 4 Termination
children.text: The Head Start agency may not terminate a delegate agency’s contract or reduce a delegate agency’s service area without showing cause or demonstrating the cost-effectiveness of such a decision.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 5 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to limit the powers, duties, or functions of the Secretary with respect to Head Start agencies or delegate agencies that receive financial assistance under this subchapter.

children.heading: Corrective action for Head Start agencies
children.label: e Corrective action for Head Start agencies
A
children.text: inform the agency of the deficiencies that shall be corrected and identify the assistance to be provided consistent with paragraph (3);

children.label: i
children.text: to correct the deficiency immediately, if the Secretary finds that the deficiency threatens the health or safety of staff or program participants or poses a threat to the integrity of Federal funds;

children.label: ii
children.text: to correct the deficiency not later than 90 days after the identification of the deficiency if the Secretary finds, in the discretion of the Secretary, that such a 90-day period is reasonable, in light of the nature and magnitude of the deficiency; or

children.label: iii
children.text: in the discretion of the Secretary (taking into consideration the seriousness of the deficiency and the time reasonably required to correct the deficiency), to comply with the requirements of paragraph (2) concerning a quality improvement plan; and

children.label: C
children.text: initiate proceedings to terminate the designation of the agency unless the agency corrects the deficiency.

children.label: I
children.text: the deficiencies to be corrected;

children.label: II
children.text: the actions to be taken to correct such deficiencies; and

children.label: III
children.text: the timetable for accomplishment of the corrective actions specified; and

children.label: ii
children.text: correct each deficiency identified, not later than the date for correction of such deficiency specified in such plan (which shall not be later than 1 year after the date the agency or Head Start program that is determined to have a deficiency received notice of the determination and of the specific deficiency to be corrected).

children.heading: Secretarial responsibility
children.label: B Secretarial responsibility
children.text: Not later than 30 days after receiving from a Head Start agency a proposed quality improvement plan pursuant to subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall either approve such proposed plan or specify the reasons why the proposed plan cannot be approved.

children.heading: Agency responsibility
children.label: C Agency responsibility
children.text: Not later than 30 days after receiving from a Head Start program a proposed quality improvement plan pursuant to subparagraph (A), the Head Start agency involved shall either approve such proposed plan or specify the reasons why the proposed plan cannot be approved.

children.heading: Training and technical assistance
children.label: 3 Training and technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary shall provide training and technical assistance to Head Start agencies and programs with respect to the development or implementation of such quality improvement plans to the extent the Secretary finds such provision to be feasible and appropriate given available funding and other statutory responsibilities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 120 days after the end of each fiscal year, the Secretary shall publish a summary report on the findings of reviews conducted under subsection (c) and on the outcomes of quality improvement plans implemented under subsection (e), during such fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: in an understandable and uniform format; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to the extent practicable, in a language that the parents understand; and

children.label: i
children.text: distribution through public agencies; and

children.label: ii
children.text: posting such information on the Internet.

children.label: A
children.text: on compliance with specific standards and measures; and

children.label: B
children.text: sufficient to allow Head Start agencies to use such data to improve the quality of their programs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not less frequently than once each program year, with the consultation and participation of policy councils and, as applicable, policy committees and, as appropriate, other community members, each Head Start agency, and each delegate agency, that receives financial assistance under this subchapter shall conduct a comprehensive self-assessment of its effectiveness and progress in meeting program goals and objectives and in implementing and complying with standards described in subsection (a)(1).

children.heading: Goals
children.label: A Goals
children.text: An agency conducting a self-assessment shall establish agency-determined program goals for improving the school readiness of children participating in a program under this subchapter, including school readiness goals that are aligned with the Head Start Child Outcomes Framework, State early learning standards as appropriate, and requirements and expectations of the schools the children will be attending.

children.heading: Improvement plan
children.label: B Improvement plan
children.text: The agency shall develop, and submit to the Secretary a report containing, an improvement plan approved by the governing body of the agency to strengthen any areas identified in the self-assessment as weaknesses or in need of improvement.

children.heading: Ongoing monitoring
children.label: 3 Ongoing monitoring
children.text: Each Head Start agency (including each Early Head Start agency) and each delegate agency shall establish and implement procedures for the ongoing monitoring of their respective programs, to ensure that the operations of the programs work toward meeting program goals and objectives and standards described in subsection (a)(1).

children.heading: Reduction of grants and redistribution of funds in cases of underenrollment
children.label: h Reduction of grants and redistribution of funds in cases of underenrollment

children.heading: Actual enrollment
children.label: A Actual enrollment
children.text: The term “actual enrollment” means, with respect to the program of a Head Start agency, the actual number of children enrolled in such program and reported by the agency (as required in paragraph (2)) in a given month.

children.heading: Base grant
children.label: B Base grant
children.text: The term “base grant” has the meaning given the term in .section 9835(a)(7) of this title

children.heading: Funded enrollment
children.label: C Funded enrollment
children.text: The term “funded enrollment” means, with respect to the program of a Head Start agency in a fiscal year, the number of children that the agency is funded to serve through a grant for the program during such fiscal year, as indicated in the grant agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: the actual enrollment in such program; and

children.label: B
children.text: if such actual enrollment is less than the funded enrollment, any apparent reason for such enrollment shortfall.

children.label: A
children.text: on a semiannual basis, determine which Head Start agencies are operating with an actual enrollment that is less than the funded enrollment based on not less than 4 consecutive months of data;

children.label: i
children.text: the quality and extent of the outreach, recruitment, and communitywide strategic planning and needs assessment conducted by such agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: changing demographics, mobility of populations, and the identification of new underserved low-income populations;

children.label: iii
children.text: facilities-related issues that may impact enrollment;

children.label: iv
children.text: the ability to provide full-working-day programs, where needed, through funds made available under this subchapter or through collaboration with entities carrying out other early childhood education and development programs, or programs with other funding sources (where available);

children.label: v
children.text: the availability and use by families of other early childhood education and development options in the community served; and

children.label: vi
children.text: agency management procedures that may impact enrollment; and

children.label: C
children.text: provide timely and ongoing technical assistance to each agency described in subparagraph (B) for the purpose of assisting the Head Start agency to implement the plan described in such subparagraph.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 4 Implementation
children.text: Upon receipt of the technical assistance described in paragraph (3)(C), a Head Start agency shall immediately implement the plan described in paragraph (3)(B). The Secretary shall, where determined appropriate, continue to provide technical assistance to such agency.

children.heading: Secretarial review and adjustment for chronic underenrollment
children.label: 5 Secretarial review and adjustment for chronic underenrollment
i
children.text: designate such agency as chronically underenrolled; and

children.label: ii
children.text: recapture, withhold, or reduce the base grant for the program by a percentage equal to the percentage difference between funded enrollment and actual enrollment for the program for the most recent year for which the agency is determined to be underenrolled under paragraph (3)(A).

children.heading: Waiver or limitation of reductions
children.label: B Waiver or limitation of reductions
i
children.text: the causes of the enrollment shortfall, or a portion of the shortfall, are related to the agency’s serving significant numbers of highly mobile children, or are other significant causes as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: the shortfall can reasonably be expected to be temporary; or

children.label: iii
children.text: the number of slots allotted to the agency is small enough that underenrollment does not create a significant shortfall.

children.heading: Indian Head Start programs
children.label: i Indian Head Start programs
children.text: If such funds are derived from an Indian Head Start program, then such funds shall be redistributed to increase enrollment by the end of the following fiscal year in 1 or more Indian Head Start programs.

children.heading: Migrant and seasonal Head Start programs
children.label: ii Migrant and seasonal Head Start programs
children.text: If such funds are derived from a migrant or seasonal Head Start program, then such funds shall be redistributed to increase enrollment by the end of the following fiscal year in 1 or more programs of the type from which such funds are derived.

children.heading: Early Head Start programs
children.label: iii Early Head Start programs
children.text: If such funds are derived from an Early Head Start program in a State, then such funds shall be redistributed to increase enrollment by the end of the following fiscal year in 1 or more Early Head Start programs in that State. If such funds are derived from an Indian Early Head Start program, then such funds shall be redistributed to increase enrollment by the end of the following fiscal year in 1 or more Indian Early Head Start programs.

children.heading: Other Head Start programs
children.label: iv Other Head Start programs
children.text: If such funds are derived from a Head Start program in a State (excluding programs described in clauses (i) through (iii)), then such funds shall be redistributed to increase enrollment by the end of the following fiscal year in 1 or more Head Start programs (excluding programs described in clauses (i) through (iii)) that are carried out in such State.

children.heading: Adjustment to funded enrollment
children.label: B Adjustment to funded enrollment
children.text: The Secretary shall adjust as necessary the requirements relating to funded enrollment indicated in the grant agreement of a Head Start agency receiving redistributed funds under this paragraph.
